text.title: csharp-entity-framework Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for csharp-entity-framework as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using OnDelete(DeleteBehavior.Cascade) on multiple paths in SQL Server without checking the migration SQL.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: SQL Server rejects FK cycles caused by multiple cascade paths, producing a migration error only at apply time, not at code review time.

failure-mode.description: Omitting RowVersion or ConcurrencyCheck on aggregate roots.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without optimistic concurrency, last-write-wins silently corrupts data under concurrent updates.

failure-mode.description: Chaining multiple Include().ThenInclude() calls for large object graphs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each additional level multiplies the result set rows. A 3-level Include on a 1000-row table can return 10^6 rows to the client.

failure-mode.description: Generated queries using FirstOrDefault(u => u.Email == email) without case normalization.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: SQL Server comparisons are case-insensitive by default but Postgres is not; .ToLower() on both sides is required for cross-provider consistency.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: EF SaveChangesAsync is already async I/O; wrapping in Task.Run adds a thread-pool context switch with no benefit and can cause DbContext threading violations.

failure-mode.description: Adding audit fields (CreatedAt, UpdatedBy) ad-hoc in every entity's configuration.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cross-cutting concerns belong in a SaveChangesInterceptor, not duplicated per entity. Duplication causes inconsistent implementation and missed fields.
